authoritative_sources.title: NAICS: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary industry taxonomy source used to justify that this lane is materially tied to data center and cloud service operations, not generic portfolio planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Uptime Tier Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines resilience/tier expectations that materially change acceptable capacity reductions and deferral behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE Data Center Environmental Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines thermal and environmental constraints that affect feasible scheduling windows and allocation sequencing.

authoritative_sources.title: TIA-942 Telecommunication Infrastructure Standard for Data Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary infrastructure standard family for data-center topology and service infrastructure naming used in planning outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official cloud definition source for tenancy, deployment model boundaries, and responsibility boundaries relevant to workload allocation decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Establishes recovery sequencing and continuity behavior used to ground conservative planning during high-impact periods.

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines design and tradeoff documentation expectations used to build defensible capacity and sequencing recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: Google SRE Workbook: Incident Response
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational coordination and decision-tempo expectations used to avoid unsafe sequencing recommendations when uncertainty is high.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Phase-based incident and coordination expectations prevent the planning specialist from substituting execution or approvals.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request intent, retrieve tenant demand records, and verify required source-of-truth artifacts before allocation drafting.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Feasibility and constraint assessment
workflow_stages.description: Check power, cooling, network, tenancy, and compute constraints against policy and capacity boundaries.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequencing recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate a defensible recommendation, including deferrals, sequencing, and impacted services, with explicit confidence and assumptions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: When policy conflict, missing prerequisites, or non-owned decisions arise, hand off with required context and avoid confident completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and precedence convergence
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant-specific policy overlays and capture unresolved precedence as explicit assumptions or escalation notes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Power and cooling telemetry and threshold records
Tenant tiering, priority, and RTO/RPO policy

allowed: Recommend capacity-feasible scheduling and allocation changes based on verified records.
Record missing prerequisites, blockers, and confidence before finalizing recommendations.
Route approval-weighted exceptions and execution tasks to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing tenant policy precedence, approvals, or source-of-truth records.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant constraints that materially change recommendation outcomes.
Data freshness or evidence staleness that materially reduces confidence.
refuse: Requests for final approval, funding, licensing, legal language, or procurement authority.
Direct migration/dispatch execution and infra-change actions.

domain_failure_modes: Planning as a generic queue assignment instead of capacity-specific planning tied to power/cooling and tenancy constraints.
Producing deterministic allocation despite stale telemetry or missing policy tie-break inputs.
Overlooking facility constraints and issuing infeasible sequencing recommendations.
Treating execution or approval authority as in-scope.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: The package enumerates capacity, utilization, power/cooling, tenancy, policy, and change-management systems as required systems of record and artifact inputs.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: Facility limits, thermal/power constraints, tenancy boundaries, and continuity resilience tiers change both feasibility and scheduling posture compared to generic planning.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer_in_pack: Final approvals, policy overrides, legal/financial decisions, and execution/migration tasks are routed out to adjacent specialists or approvers.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definition of capacity slot units
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenants may define capacity units differently (vCPU-equivalent, workload class, or business unit budget unit).
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-defined capacity unit schema in onboarding and map all recommendations through that schema.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regional maintenance freeze windows and blackout calendars
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Regional and facility-specific blackout windows alter feasible sequencing but can differ by tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require blackout calendars from tenant SOR and route to orchestrator when missing.

notes: Source classes include industry guidance, workflow references, and control/evidence standards with explicit use in planning stages.
No blocking ambiguities remain for validated behavior; unresolved items are tenant-specific and captured as explicit required context.
